content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660              322-5901           322-6441          322-6444

June 13, 1980

Mr. Joe Morgan
CCPI
14442 E. Whittier Blvd., Suite J
P.O. Box 4877
Whittier, CA 90607

Dear Mr. Morgan:

     This letter is in response to your letter to the Fair
Political Practices Commission in which you request an extension
of time to file and permission to combine two campaign statements
required under Government Code Section 84204.

     The Political Reform Act does not provide the Commission or
any other responsible agency with the authority to grant filing
deadline extensions or to allow statements required under this
section to be combined.

     Based on the information provided in your letter concerning
petition circulation deadlines a campaign statement in connection
with your first circulation effort should have been filed no
later than 35 days after April 28, 1980. This report should
include all money raised and spent in connection with the first
initiative. The next report will be required no later than 35
days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of no-
tification that the measure has qualified or failed to qualify
whichever is earlier.

     Please do not hesitate to contact this office if you have
any additional questions.

                                        Very)Truly Yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
                                        Political Reform Consultant II

Californians
for a Competitive
Petroleum
Industry

June 6, 1980

Tom Sobel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K St.
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR EXTENSION OF TIME

This letter is to request authorization to file a single report for
our campaign. The purpose for this request is based upon the following
set of circumstances surrounding our initiative drive.

We initially filed our initiative in October 1979, and received our
title and summary from the Attorney General on November 28, 1979.
The 150 day period was due to expire on April 28, 1980.

However, due to internal organization problems we decided to abandon
our efforts in collecting signatures and consequently informed the
Secretary of State and the Attorney General of our intent on or
about January 21, 1980.

On or about January 22, 1980, we refiled our initiative with the
Attorney General and received a new title and summary from the
Attorney General on or about March 1, 1980. Our 150 day period for
circulating our petitions is due to expire on or about August 1, 1980.

Since it would be difficult to separate the expenditures of our first
effort from the second, we respectfully request that we be permitted
to file a single report with your office on or before September 5,
1980.

That deadline would be in compliance with the 35 day reporting
requirement after our August 1, 1980, date for filing our petitions.

We will fully comply with the provisions of the Fair Political Practices
Act, and ask favorable consideration for our request.

Cordially,

CALIFORNIANS FOR A COMPETITIVE PETROLEUM INDUSTRY

Joe B. Morgan

Joe B. Morgan

JBM/al

Joe B. Morgan, Chairman
14442 E. WHITTIER BLVD., SUITE J
P.O. BOX 4877
WHITTIER, CA.90607
(213) 945-2927

Paid for by Californians for a Competitive Petroleum Industry, I.D. # 790839

sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not provide the Commission or any other responsible agency with the authority to grant filing deadline extensions or to allow statements required under this section to be combined. A campaign statement in connection with the first circulation effort should have been filed no later than 35 days after April 28, 1980, including all money raised and spent in connection with the first initiative. The next report will be required no later than 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of notification that the measure has qualified or failed to qualify, whichever is earlier.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC can grant an extension of time to file campaign statements under Government Code Section 84204 and whether two required campaign statements can be combined.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot grant filing deadline extensions or allow required campaign statements to be combined under the Political Reform Act. The campaign statement for the first circulation effort was due no later than 35 days after April 28, 1980, and the next report is due 35 days after the petition filing deadline or qualification notification, whichever is earlier.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; extracted from letter body. Dated June 13, 1980. Addressed to Joe Morgan at CCPI regarding campaign statement filing requirements for initiative petition circulation efforts. Letter signed by Regina Siciliano-Kutchins, Political Reform Consultant II.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the FPPC can grant an extension of time to file campaign statements under Government Code Section 84204 and whether two required campaign statements can be combined.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not provide the Commission or any other responsible agency with the authority to grant filing deadline extensions or to allow statements required under this section to be combined. A campaign statement in connection with the first circulation effort should have been filed no later than 35 days after April 28, 1980, including all money raised and spent in connection with the first initiative. The next report will be required no later than 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the date of notification that the measure has qualified or failed to qualify, whichever is earlier.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to a request for filing deadline extension and permission to combine campaign statements related to initiative petition circulation under Government Code Section 84204. The Commission denies both requests, explaining it lacks authority to grant extensions or allow statement combinations.
